title: The Simurgh Quest
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A disgraced royal cartographer and a warrior woman must cross the Seven Valleys of Persian mysticism to find the Simurgh, the primordial bird whose feather alone can save a dying Shah.
long_description: When Shahanshah Khosrow of Persia is struck by a mysterious wasting curse, the court astrologers declare that only a feather from the Simurgh — the primordial bird of Persian myth — can save him. The task falls to Rostam Karimi, a disgraced royal cartographer who once mapped the empire's lost borders and now seeks redemption after a failed rebellion cost him his rank and his family. He is joined by Parisa Daneshvar, a warrior woman of the Jeyan tribe whose village was burned on the Shah's orders, and who sees the quest as a path to a different kind of justice. Guided by the cryptic predictions of the Zoroastrian priest Darius Kamgar, the pair must cross the Seven Valleys of Persian mysticism — each a literal and spiritual trial — while the Shah's ambitious vizier, Asmodeus, dispatches assassins to ensure the Simurgh never heals the king he intends to replace. The quest becomes a confrontation with their own past sins, the nature of power, and the truth behind the myth.
